title: Harness heartbeat line
description: One JSON object per line in heartbeat.ndjson. The worker contract (FR-18): a job is anything that appends these lines to a file. `status` is the ONLY field the harness interprets (it drives the state machine); everything else is decoration the harness displays but never reads. `label` is a single short free-form string shown verbatim in the status table (column ACTIVITY; truncated to width and ASCII-sanitized for DISPLAY only, raw preserved on disk) — it replaces the v1 `phase`/`step` pair. `data` is an opaque JSON object the harness never reads (the structure escape hatch / A2A path). schema_version is '2'; the harness honors Postel's law and still reads v1 lines (`phase`/`step`, schema_version '1') — be conservative in what it emits, liberal in what it accepts; a malformed line is skipped with a warning, never fatal. QPB's vendored integration sets `label` from its own phase identity (e.g. '2:generation') and carries the old step under `data.step`; that coupling never enters the generic core. This file is byte-identical to plugins/quality-playbook/skills/quality-playbook/schemas/heartbeat.schema.json — the cross-skill byte-equality contract is load-bearing (Council C-3 silent-drift risk) and pinned by bin/tests/test_harness_schemas.py.

ts.description: ISO8601 UTC timestamp (Zulu, second precision). Append-only ordering invariant: later lines have ts >= earlier lines in the same file.

task_id.description: Task id matching the job manifest. Threaded from the worker prompt header (TASK_ID=<id>) or env var HARNESS_TASK_ID / QPB_TASK_ID.

schema_version.description: Schema-version pin. Current emit version is '2' (label/data). v1 (phase/step) is still accepted on read (Postel). The harness warns on any OTHER value (Council C-3 drift).

label.description: v2: a single short free-form activity string, displayed verbatim in the ACTIVITY column (truncated to column width and ASCII-sanitized for display; raw preserved on disk). Optional. Replaces the v1 phase/step pair. The harness never interprets it.

phase.description: v1 (legacy, read-only): the old canonical phase slug. Still accepted on read for back-compatibility; new lines use `label`.

step.description: v1 (legacy, read-only): the old step within a phase. Still accepted on read; new lines fold any equivalent under `data`.

status.description: The ONLY field the harness interprets. Progress: STARTING / IN_PROGRESS / COMPLETED / FAILED. Terminal sentinel (last line): COMPLETED / FAILED / ABANDONED.

data.description: Optional opaque JSON object the harness NEVER reads — the structure escape hatch and A2A migration path. A worker may carry any side payload here (QPB stashes the legacy step under data.step).

result_file.description: Path of the terminal result file (quality/SUMMARY.md or equivalent). Present on the terminal sentinel; absent on progress lines.

summary.description: One-line outcome. Present on the terminal sentinel; absent on progress lines.
